title: Vanishing Index Table
hypothesis: Vanishing Index Table tests whether archive loss can be felt through irreversible filing decisions instead of decorative particles.
dataModel: seeded table rows with missing labels, variable row counts, and irreversible rescued cells; mutation:custody-shift
recordCountStrategy: seeded variable 12-25 records with no constant-size record array
interactionModel: click and pointer-scrub cells to rescue or misfile them; state changes row classes and counts; mutation mode custody-shift
stateModel: localStorage stores recovered, locked, and misfiled record ids for this draft id
visualLanguage: dense tabular archive with gaps, stamps, and offset labels
expectedStructuralChange: Interaction must change at least one record position, class, label, count, and persisted state.
